title: galium boreale subsp. linearifolium
intro: Galium boreale subsp. linearifolium, also known as the linear-leaved bedstraw, is a subspecies of the Galium boreale plant, a type of flowering plant in the bedstraw family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The linear-leaved bedstraw has been found to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant can be dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
